title: pittosporum nervosum
intro: Pittosporum nervosum, also known as the New Zealand tobacco tree, is a small to medium-sized tree native to New Zealand with a history of traditional medicinal use. The plant's leaves and seeds have been used in herbal medicine for their expectorant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Consuming Pittosporum nervosum is believed to provide various health benefits, including improved respiratory function, relief from allergies, and antioxidant effects. In traditional herbal preparations, the leaves and seeds are often made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
